completed: Fetched origin/main and verified clean local main matches the remote baseline
Selected R90-14 as the highest-priority dependency-ready increment
Confirmed the initial C connection sends hello before packet and heartbeat traffic
Confirmed the UDS_ERR_PIPE reconnect path opens a replacement connection without resending hello
Recorded the compatibility impact and explicit unblock condition in the rolling roadmap
todo: Obtain product authority to change the C reconnect lifecycle so every replacement connection sends hello before traffic, or approve another explicit compatibility contract
After unblocking, implement the receiver state machine and all direct rejection, reconnect, capacity, cancellation, E2E, and delivery validation

blocker.summary: The checked-in C reconnect path does not satisfy hello-first ordering on replacement connections
evidence: capture/src/main.c packet_handler calls uds_reconnect after UDS_ERR_PIPE without calling uds_send_hello
capture/src/uds_sender.c uds_reconnect only opens the replacement socket
blocker.impact: Receiver-only enforcement would close a valid checked-in capture reconnect on its next packet or heartbeat
blocker.unblock_condition: Authorize the C reconnect-handshake change with direct C and E2E coverage, or define another explicit compatibility contract

remaining_risks: Connection session state must remain local across concurrent clients
Every state violation must close only its connection and increment decode errors once
resume_instructions: R90-14 is blocked before implementation. Do not enforce hello-first receiver behavior until the reconnect compatibility decision is authorized. After authority is recorded, update the C reconnect path or the explicit compatibility contract, then complete the plan validation. Do not create v0.1.1 or publish artifacts.
